stories.headline: Foxconn Hands Its Factories an AI Brain: Inside “MoMClaw,” the Multi-Agent System Built on NVIDIA's FOX Blueprint
stories.key_takeaway: Foxconn's MoMClaw system, built on NVIDIA's new FOX blueprint, deploys hundreds of coordinated AI agents across live production lines, with Foxconn projecting roughly 80% faster root-cause analysis and about 10% fewer machine failures.

pull_quote.text: If you think about time to revenue, factory setup, and factory planning, we believe that we can cut that down by about 50%.
pull_quote.role: General Manager, Fii Robotic Group, Foxconn

stories.article_excerpt: Foxconn is wiring hundreds of AI agents into its production lines through a new system called MoMClaw — projecting ~80% faster fault diagnosis.

stories.headline: NewLimit Raises $435M to Push AI-Designed Age-Reversal Medicine Toward Its First Human Trial
stories.key_takeaway: NewLimit closed a $435M Series C led by Founders Fund at a ~$3.1B valuation, with its AI-driven cellular reprogramming work advancing a liver age-reversal medicine toward a first human trial next year.

pull_quote.text: Defying prior dogma, this emerging science has demonstrated that aging is plastic at the cellular level.

stories.article_excerpt: A biotech betting that aging can be reprogrammed at the cellular level just got one of the largest war chests in longevity.

stories.headline: Salesforce Buys Contentful to Give Agentforce a Content Brain
stories.key_takeaway: Salesforce will acquire Contentful, reportedly for $1-1.5 billion, to give its Agentforce agents a native headless content layer for assembling personalized experiences at scale.

pull_quote.text: With Contentful, we complete that picture by adding a native, headless, composable content layer that lets Agentforce dynamically assemble and deliver personalized experiences across every channel.

stories.article_excerpt: Salesforce has agreed to buy Contentful, the headless content platform used by 4,800+ brands, to give Agentforce a content layer.

stories.headline: Asana Buys StackAI to Give Its AI Agents the Power to Actually Do the Work
stories.key_takeaway: Asana acquired no-code agent-builder StackAI (reportedly ~$75M) to let its AI agents execute work directly across enterprise systems, joining a vendor-wide race to own the AI execution layer.

pull_quote.text: StackAI allows them to 'agentify' more complex business processes end-to-end across the systems and tools their businesses run on.

stories.article_excerpt: Asana has agreed to acquire StackAI, a no-code AI-agent platform, pushing the work-management vendor from organizing work to executing it.

stories.headline: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Push Autonomous Agents From the Desktop to the Data Center
stories.key_takeaway: NVIDIA and ServiceNow expanded their partnership at Knowledge 2026 to deliver governed autonomous AI agents spanning employee desktops to data-center AI factories, anchored by the Project Arc desktop agent on NVIDIA's OpenShell runtime.

pull_quote.text: Long-running, autonomous agents are rapidly changing the game for enterprise AI, and delivering them securely at scale requires governance that spans models, software and AI infrastructure.
pull_quote.role: VP of Generative AI for Enterprise, NVIDIA

data_points.label: Token output per watt, Blackwell vs Hopper

stories.article_excerpt: At ServiceNow Knowledge 2026, NVIDIA and ServiceNow set out to answer the harder enterprise question: how should AI act, and who is accountable?

stories.headline: DiffusionGemma: Google DeepMind Bets on Text Diffusion for 4x Faster Local Generation
stories.key_takeaway: Google DeepMind's experimental, Apache 2.0-licensed DiffusionGemma uses parallel text diffusion instead of token-by-token decoding to run up to 4x faster on local GPUs, trading some output quality for speed.

pull_quote.text: While autoregressive Gemma 4 models remain the standard for high-quality production outputs, DiffusionGemma is designed for researchers and developers exploring speed-critical, interactive local workflows.

stories.article_excerpt: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, an experimental open model that drafts whole text blocks at once instead of word-by-word.

stories.headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Is a Frontier “Mythos-Class” Model — Not the Creative-Writing Specialist Some Expected
stories.key_takeaway: Despite a name that suggested a cheap creative-writing companion, Claude Fable 5 is Anthropic's most capable generally available model — a safeguarded, frontier 'Mythos-class' model priced above Opus.

pull_quote.text: Today we're launching Claude Fable 5: a Mythos-class model that we've made safe for general use. Fable 5's capabilities exceed those of any model we've ever made generally available.

stories.article_excerpt: When Anthropic teased a model called 'Fable,' the name suggested a storytelling engine. The model that shipped is almost the opposite.

stories.headline: A $1.25-an-Hour Frontier Model? Why “Orion-100B” Deserves Scrutiny Before Applause
stories.key_takeaway: The viral 'Orion-100B at $1.25/hour' claim could not be verified against any primary source and should be treated cautiously, even though the broader trend of cheap decentralized large-model training is real.

pull_quote.text: 'Orion-100B at $1.25/hour' is, for now, a claim in search of a source — and the right posture is skepticism until the receipts arrive.

stories.article_excerpt: A claim making the rounds would, if true, rewrite the economics of building LLMs. After checking, it doesn't hold up to verification.

stories.headline: Michigan's Prima AI Reads a Brain MRI in Seconds, Hitting 97.5% Accuracy Across 50+ Conditions
stories.key_takeaway: University of Michigan's Prima, a vision-language AI trained on 200,000+ brain MRIs, diagnosed neurological conditions in seconds with up to 97.5% accuracy and flagged urgent cases like strokes and hemorrhages.

pull_quote.text: As the global demand for MRI rises and places significant strain on our physicians and health systems, our AI model has potential to reduce burden by improving diagnosis and treatment with fast, accurate information.

stories.article_excerpt: A brain MRI can take days to read. Michigan researchers built an AI that compresses that wait to seconds.

stories.headline: The MIT Startup That Wants to Cool AI's Chips With No Water and a Nuclear Reactor's Playbook
stories.key_takeaway: MIT-founded startup Ferveret adapts a nuclear-reactor cooling technique to cool AI chips with no water and less electricity, claiming 15% better power efficiency and 35% more AI output per watt.

pull_quote.text: The sun shines in places where you don't have much water, so the advantage of us being water-free is we allow you to build data centers where you have solar energy but nothing to cool the data center down.

data_points.label: Better power efficiency vs liquid cooling

stories.article_excerpt: The AI boom has a thermodynamics problem. An MIT spinout thinks the fix has been hiding inside nuclear reactors all along.

stories.headline: AI Promised to Help Us Spot Fake News. An MIT Study Says It Quietly Made Us Worse.
stories.key_takeaway: An MIT Media Lab study of 67 people found AI chatbots made users 21% better at spotting fake news in the moment, but left their unassisted accuracy 15 points lower after a month — a cognitive-offloading effect akin to GPS dulling navigation.

pull_quote.text: Users get excited about these 'magical' LLMs, but forget that they're just statistical models that predict the next 'token' in a sequence.
pull_quote.role: PhD student and co-lead author, MIT Media Lab

stories.article_excerpt: A new MIT Media Lab study adds an unsettling entry to the ledger of skills we are outsourcing to machines: spotting fake news.

stories.headline: A Faster Way to See the World in Motion: D4RT Takes CVPR 2026's Top Honor
stories.key_takeaway: D4RT, a Google DeepMind, UCL and Oxford method that reconstructs the geometry and motion of dynamic 4D scenes from video using a single unified transformer, won the CVPR 2026 Best Paper award.

pull_quote.text: From advances in dynamic scene reconstruction to breakthroughs in 3D generative modeling, these works address fundamental challenges in computer vision while opening new possibilities across AI, robotics, and more.

stories.article_excerpt: Understanding a 3D world that is also changing through time is brutally hard for machines. D4RT pushes that frontier furthest this year.

stories.headline: China Drafts a $295 Billion Bet to Wire the Nation for AI
stories.key_takeaway: China is drafting a roughly $295 billion, five-year plan to build a state-operated national AI data center grid powered at least 80% by domestic chips, aiming to lock out Nvidia and AMD and link facilities by 2028.

pull_quote.text: China is preparing one of the largest state-directed digital infrastructure programmes in history, committing $295 billion over five years to build a network of AI data centres operated by its state telecoms carriers.

stories.article_excerpt: China is preparing to spend roughly $295 billion over five years to build a nationwide network of interconnected AI data centers.

stories.headline: Anthropic Calls for a Global AI “Pause” — Then Ships Its Most Powerful Model
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic urged a coordinated, verifiable global slowdown of frontier AI citing recursive self-improvement risk, then days later released its most powerful public model, Claude Fable 5 — fueling debate over whether the safety call is sincere or self-interested.

pull_quote.text: It would be good for the world to have the option to slow or temporarily pause its development.

stories.article_excerpt: Within five days, Anthropic urged a coordinated global slowdown of frontier AI — then shipped its most powerful model to anyone with an API key.

stories.headline: Pentagon Tests OpenAI and Google Models to Replace Anthropic's Claude in Classified Systems
stories.key_takeaway: The Pentagon is testing OpenAI, Google and xAI models with about 25 of its AI power users to replace Anthropic's Claude in classified systems, after a dispute over Anthropic's safety guardrails escalated into a supply-chain-risk designation now being fought in court.

pull_quote.text: Punishing Anthropic for bringing public scrutiny to the government's contracting position is classic illegal First Amendment retaliation.

stories.article_excerpt: The Pentagon is evaluating AI models from OpenAI, Google and xAI as potential replacements for Anthropic's Claude in classified systems.
